title: Verilator ignores object file when using `--lib-create`
body:
I'm using `rustc` to compile a rust file into an object file which I then want to link into a shared object library
```
 "verilator" "--cc" "-sv" "--build" "-j" "0" "-CFLAGS" "-shared -fpic" "--lib-create" "dumbname_Vmain" "--Mdir" "artifacts/main/obj_dir" "--top-module" "main" "sv/dpi.sv" "../ffi/ffi.cpp" "../dpi/dpi.o" "../dpi/wrappers.cpp"
```
`dpi.o` is the rust file I make
```
"rustc" "--emit=obj" "--crate-type=cdylib" "--edition" "2021" "dpi.rs"
```
verilator is interpreting my `.o` as for LDFLAGS:
```
# User LDLIBS (from -LDFLAGS on Verilator command line)
VM_USER_LDLIBS = \
    ../dpi/dpi.o \
```
```
[I] ~/g/d/v/example-project (dpi|âśš5) [SIGABRT]$ nm artifacts/main/obj_dir/libdumbname_Vmain.so | rg three
0000000000004110 T __Z50Vmain___024unit____Vdpiimwrap_three_TOP____024unitRj
0000000000004668 T __Z5threePj
                 U _rust_three
                 U _three
[I] ~/g/d/v/example-project (dpi|âśš5) $ nm artifacts/main/dpi/dpi.o
0000000000000000 T _rust_three
0000000000000000 t ltmp0
0000000000000010 s ltmp1
```
Full code:
- https://github.com/ethanuppal/dumbname/blob/dpi/verilog-support/example-project/sv/dpi.sv
- https://github.com/ethanuppal/dumbname/blob/dpi/verilog-support/example-project/src/dpi_tutorial.rs

What 'verilator' command line do we use to run your example?

See above

What 'verilator --version' are you using?  Did you try it with the git master version?

Verilator 5.025 devel rev UNKNOWN.REV, and No

What OS and distribution are you using?

macOS 14.2.1 arm64

May we assist you in trying to fix this in Verilator yourself?

Sure
code_blocks:
"verilator" "--cc" "-sv" "--build" "-j" "0" "-CFLAGS" "-shared -fpic" "--lib-create" "dumbname_Vmain" "--Mdir" "artifacts/main/obj_dir" "--top-module" "main" "sv/dpi.sv" "../ffi/ffi.cpp" "../dpi/dpi.o" "../dpi/wrappers.cpp"
"rustc" "--emit=obj" "--crate-type=cdylib" "--edition" "2021" "dpi.rs"
# User LDLIBS (from -LDFLAGS on Verilator command line)
VM_USER_LDLIBS = \
    ../dpi/dpi.o \
